What we heard from discovery:
- Primary use: real-time payments routing + fraud scoring for API-driven flows.
- Expected peak: ~500 requests/sec (burst to 900qps for 30s) with avg payload ~1.2k tokens.

- Data handling: customer PII redaction required; will need VPC/private deployment conversation for production.

From: Alex Johnson <alex@keplercommerce.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Priya Singh <priya@keplercommerce.com>
Cc: Danielle Ortiz <danielle@keplercommerce.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:12:43 -0700
Subject: Sudden spike in latency and 5xxs during Thursday peak

Ben —

We saw a sharp degradation during Thursday afternoon peak (approx 16:10–16:40 UTC): 95th pctl latency jumped from ~220ms to ~1.8s and 5xx rate climbed from baseline 0.02% to ~1.7%. This affected checkout flows and mobile app API requests. Our customers reported timeouts and cart failures for ~30 minutes.

Attached is a small excerpt of logs and a few request IDs (sample IDs: kp-7a1, kp-7a2, kp-7a3) that show repeated 503s. Can you confirm whether this is on Redwood side? We need an executive readout for our CTO and ops lead — please advise next steps and estimated ETA for root cause and mitigation.

Thanks,
Alex

title: Overlap in maintenance attribution & probe fingerprint mismatch
preview:
From: Samantha Ruiz <samantha@cloudwave.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>
Cc: cloudwave-ops@cloudwave.com
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Observed uptime discrepancy during 05/12 maintenance window

Jonas,

We ran our reconciliation for May and noticed that the availability ledger shows a 0.9% u...
text:
From: Samantha Ruiz <samantha@cloudwave.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>
Cc: cloudwave-ops@cloudwave.com
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Observed uptime discrepancy during 05/12 maintenance window

Jonas,

We ran our reconciliation for May and noticed that the availability ledger shows a 0.9% uptime hit attributed to Redwood for the 05/12 03:00-04:30 UTC maintenance window. Our records indicate our maintenance window was 03:00-03:45 and the downstream provider (EdgeRoute) had a 03:40-04:00 maintenance — there appears to be an overlap that your calculation attributed fully to Redwood.

Can you share the exact attribution rules you used? In particular, our probe traces show traffic recovered at 03:46 but a series of synthetic probes timed out again at 04:15 — we're concerned those later timeouts were caused by EdgeRoute's concurrent change and not Redwood.

We need...

Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Multi-tenant throttle spike — coordinated mitigation & customer note

Team —

We just saw a noticeable p95/p99 latency increase and a ~30% sustained throughput drop for a multi-tenant group in us-west-2 starting ~08:50 PDT. The initial correlation points to an aggressive kernel scheduling backoff plus cross-tenant batching interactions; Streamline Labs is one of the impacted customers (they're in prod and report user-facing errors).

Immediate asks:
- Karthik: can you triage kernel scheduling / priority-queue behaviour and confirm if we can temporarily raise Streamline's priority or isolate them on a sep...

text:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Daniel Park <daniel.park@helixsys.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-09T09:00:00-07:00
Subject: Request: Exec sign-off — 5yr Stability Covenant + Price Protection for Helix Systems

Hi Daniel,

Thanks again for the productive call earlier. Per our conversation, Helix is evaluating a 5-year committed package (reserved capacity + hosted API for mission-critical workloads). They asked for multi-year pricing protection and clear renewal mechanics — specifically: a price lock for the first 36 months, then a modest uplift cap thereafter, and contractual renewal protection so their unit economics aren’t materially degraded year-over-year.

text:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: URGENT: Bluehaven latency spikes — prep customer comms and relief proposal

Team —

Quick summary: Bluehaven reported sustained tail-latency spikes across their /generate route from 08:05–08:48 PDT (they're on us-west). Modeled traffic shows a kernel-level memory fragmentation issue that led to increased GC overhead on our dedicated cluster serving their tenant. Engineering has a live trace and pre-lim heap dump attached.

Impact: ~5% of requests returned >3s LAT after 08:20 for a 45-minute window. Customer reported several failed workflows. They flagged billing sensitivity and have asked for an executive note + proposal for service relief (credit) while we remediate.

Asks:
- Stephanie: can you run a sh...
